Great Canadian Property Services — Full Site Content Summary
============================================================
URL: https://www.greatcanadianpropertyservices.ca/
Phone: 604-260-6285
Email: info@greatcanadianpropertyservices.ca
Address: 112-15428 31 Ave, Surrey, BC V3Z 3W4
Google Rating: 5.0 stars
Coverage: 14 communities across Greater Vancouver, Fraser Valley, and Sea to Sky corridor
Fully insured and licensed. All property assessments are complimentary and no-obligation.


ABOUT THE COMPANY
-----------------
Great Canadian Property Services is a Greater Vancouver exterior maintenance company specialising in pressure washing, soft-wash house washing, roof cleaning, window cleaning, concrete and paver care, and custom holiday lighting. The company serves both residential homeowners and commercial property managers, strata corporations, and industrial facilities. Service is characterised by meticulous workmanship, eco-considered methods, and reliable communication. The team provides recurring maintenance programmes as well as one-time visits, with custom scheduling to minimise disruption to tenants and occupants.


RESIDENTIAL SERVICES
--------------------

House Washing
URL: /residential-pressure-washing/house-washing/
Soft-wash exterior washing for residential homes across Greater Vancouver. The process uses low-pressure water combined with biodegradable cleaning solutions to lift algae, mildew, lichen, and accumulated grime from vinyl siding, cedar shakes, stucco, Hardie board, brick, and painted wood without surface damage. Appropriate pressure and solution concentration are selected based on the cladding type. The result is a clean, restored exterior that improves curb appeal and protects the surface from accelerated deterioration caused by biological growth.

Roof Cleaning
URL: /residential-pressure-washing/roof-cleaning/
Safe removal of moss, lichen, algae, and gloeocapsa magma from residential rooflines. Gloeocapsa magma is a cyanobacterium that deteriorates asphalt shingles and composite roofing over time, causing premature granule loss and reducing the effective lifespan of the roof. Soft-wash treatments apply biodegradable biocide solutions at low pressure, eliminating growth without damaging shingles or underlying membranes. The service is also effective on metal, tile, and flat commercial roofing systems. Routine cleaning extends roof life and maintains the appearance of the roofline.

Window Cleaning
URL: /residential-pressure-washing/window-cleaning/ and /window-cleaning/
Interior and exterior window cleaning using two methods: water-fed pole systems and traditional squeegee technique. Water-fed poles deliver purified, mineral-free water through brush heads to dissolve surface deposits without leaving spots or streaks. Traditional squeegee work is used for interior glass, skylights, and areas where pole access is limited. Most residential properties benefit from twice-yearly cleaning cycles in coastal BC due to pollen accumulation in spring and salt air and rainfall residue in autumn and winter.

Driveway Cleaning
URL: /residential-pressure-washing/driveway-cleaning/
High-pressure cleaning of residential driveways and parking surfaces to remove oil stains, tire rubber marks, organic matter, mildew, and embedded grime. Surface-specific pressure settings are used to clean without etching concrete or damaging asphalt. Pre-treatment degreasers are applied to oil spots before pressure washing to improve stain removal. Post-cleaning, driveways can proceed to sealing for long-term surface protection.

Patio Cleaning
URL: /residential-pressure-washing/patio-cleaning/
Exterior cleaning for residential patios, decks, and outdoor living areas across all surface materials: natural stone, stamped concrete, clay pavers, wood, and composite decking. Each material requires a specific pressure setting and, where appropriate, a suitable cleaning solution. The goal is to restore surface appearance, remove slippery organic growth, and prepare surfaces for sealing or staining if required.

Concrete Cleaning
URL: /residential-pressure-washing/concrete-cleaning/
Pressure washing of concrete driveways, walkways, garage floors, porches, and retaining walls. Removes oil, algae, mildew, mould, paint overspray, and general grime. A surface cleaner attachment is used on large flat areas to ensure even pressure distribution and streak-free results. Pre-treatment for oil and rust staining is applied where necessary.

Concrete & Paver Sanding
URL: /residential-pressure-washing/concrete-paver-sanding/
Polymeric jointing sand application for interlocking brick and concrete paver installations. Over time, joint sand erodes from rain and foot traffic, leading to paver instability, weed infiltration, and insect activity. Restoration begins with pressure washing to clear joints, followed by careful application and compaction of polymeric sand. The sand hardens with moisture to form a semi-flexible bond that resists washout and inhibits weed growth. Recommended as a follow-up to paver cleaning or sealing.

Concrete & Paver Sealing
URL: /residential-pressure-washing/concrete-paver-sealing/
Application of penetrating or film-forming sealers to concrete and paver surfaces after cleaning. Sealers protect against UV fading, moisture penetration, staining, efflorescence, and organic growth. Film-forming (topical) sealers enhance colour and sheen; penetrating sealers provide invisible protection while preserving the natural look. Sealed surfaces are significantly easier to maintain and resist staining from oil, plant matter, and seasonal debris. Recommended every two to four years depending on surface type and exposure.


COMMERCIAL SERVICES
-------------------

Building Washing
URL: /commercial-pressure-washing/building-washing/
Exterior cleaning of commercial buildings, office towers, retail centres, and strata complexes using soft-wash and pressure-wash techniques appropriate for each facade material. Services are scheduled to minimise disruption to tenants and business operations. Recurring programmes available on quarterly, biannual, or custom schedules. Documented service reports provided for strata and property management records.

Commercial Window Cleaning
URL: /commercial-pressure-washing/window-cleaning/
Storefront, lobby, and multi-storey window cleaning for commercial properties. Water-fed pole systems are used for exterior glass up to five storeys; traditional squeegee technique for interior and ground-level glass. Recurring subscription programmes available — property managers set the cleaning frequency and the team handles scheduling and execution. Consistent results with minimal disruption to tenants and customers.

Fleet Washing
URL: /commercial-pressure-washing/fleet-washing/
Scheduled exterior washing for company vehicle fleets including delivery vans, service trucks, heavy equipment, and passenger vehicles. Consistent fleet presentation reinforces brand image and supports preventive maintenance by removing road salt, grime, and corrosive deposits. Programmes are configured around fleet size, vehicle type, and desired cleaning frequency. On-site service available at fleet yards or commercial facilities.

Gutter Cleaning
URL: /commercial-pressure-washing/gutter-cleaning/
Full gutter clearing for commercial properties including debris removal from gutters and downspouts, flushing to confirm drainage, and exterior brightening of gutter faces. Blocked gutters cause water to back up against the building envelope, contributing to fascia rot, siding damage, foundation issues, and interior leaks. Commercial gutter cleaning is recommended at least twice yearly in the Pacific Northwest due to heavy leaf fall and rainfall.

Industrial Pressure Washing
URL: /commercial-pressure-washing/industrial-pressure-washing/
Heavy-duty pressure washing for industrial and manufacturing environments including warehouses, loading docks, equipment yards, processing floors, and exterior hard surfaces. Industrial cleaning involves higher pressure ratings, commercial-grade detergents, and equipment suited to large surface areas and heavy contamination. Scheduling is arranged to work around operational hours and safety requirements.


TOP-LEVEL SERVICES
------------------

Window Cleaning (Top Level)
URL: /window-cleaning/
Residential and commercial window cleaning across Greater Vancouver. Covers all property types from single-family homes to multi-storey commercial buildings. Water-fed pole system delivers purified water for exterior glass; traditional squeegee for interior surfaces. Twice-yearly residential programmes recommended for coastal BC. Commercial recurring programmes available.

Holiday Lighting
URL: /christmas-lighting/
Full-service custom holiday and Christmas lighting for residential properties and commercial buildings. Service includes design consultation, custom light selection and configuration, professional installation on rooflines, eaves, trees, pathways, and landscape features, mid-season maintenance visits, and careful removal and storage after the holiday season. Bookings open in September; October bookings fill first for peak dates. No ladders or tangled strings for the homeowner — the team handles everything from the first conversation to final storage.


SERVICE AREAS
-------------

Vancouver — /near-me/vancouver-bc-pressure-washing/
From West Side heritage homes to East Vancouver craftsmen, the full range of residential and commercial exterior care. Dense urban properties benefit from discreet scheduling and professional-grade equipment.

Surrey — /near-me/surrey-bc-pressure-washing/
Home base of Great Canadian Property Services. Comprehensive residential, commercial, and strata care across South Surrey, Cloverdale, Fleetwood, Clayton Heights, and Guildford. Shortest response times in the region.

North Vancouver — /near-me/north-vancouver-bc-pressure-washing/
North Shore properties contend with heavy rainfall, significant moss growth, and shade from mature trees. Roof cleaning and house washing cycles are often shorter here than elsewhere in the region.

West Vancouver — /near-me/west-vancouver-bc-pressure-washing/
Waterfront homes, hillside estates, and forested properties. Discreet and meticulous exterior maintenance with attention to high-value surfaces and landscaping.

Richmond — /near-me/richmond-bc-pressure-washing/
Low-lying coastal conditions mean high humidity and regular algae growth on siding and concrete. From Steveston waterfront homes to Terra Nova and commercial properties along No. 3 Road.

Coquitlam — /near-me/coquitlam-bc-pressure-washing/
Tri-Cities residential and strata care from the Fraser River flats to Burke Mountain. Mix of established neighbourhoods and newer townhome developments.

Delta — /near-me/delta-bc-pressure-washing/
Tsawwassen, Ladner, and North Delta. Coastal wind and salt air accelerate surface deterioration — regular washing cycles protect siding and extend surface life.

Langley — /near-me/langley-bc-pressure-washing/
From large-lot acreage in Walnut Grove to townhome communities in Willoughby and Murrayville. Exterior care scaled to every property type.

White Rock — /near-me/white-rock-bc-pressure-washing/
Marine Drive bluff homes and beachfront properties. Salt air and sea spray are hard on painted surfaces and glass. Frequent window cleaning and house washing recommended.

Maple Ridge — /near-me/maple-ridge-bc-pressure-washing/
Properties along the Fraser River and at the base of the Coast Mountains. Acreages, family homes, and newer developments served with the same standard of care.

Abbotsford — /near-me/abbotsford-bc-pressure-washing/
Residential and agricultural exterior care across the heart of the Fraser Valley. Mix of suburban homes and rural properties with larger surface areas.

Chilliwack — /near-me/chilliwack-bc-pressure-washing/
Eastern Fraser Valley residential, agricultural, and commercial properties. Mountain backdrop and valley climate mean heavy seasonal organic growth on roofs and siding.

Squamish — /near-me/squamish-bc-pressure-washing/
Sea to Sky exterior maintenance built for high winds, heavy rain, and dramatic seasonal conditions. Properties in Squamish experience some of the most intense weather in the service area.

Whistler — /near-me/whistler-bc-pressure-washing/
Mountain residences, chalets, and resort properties. Year-round exterior care including snow-season window cleaning and pre-season prep for spring and summer.


ARTICLES
--------

How Often Should You Clean Your Home's Windows in a Rainy Coastal Climate?
URL: /pressure-washing-articles/how-often-should-you-clean-your-homes-windows-in-a-rainy-coastal-climate/
Coastal weather brings steady rain, sea air, and drifting pollen. Despite frequent rainfall, windows do not self-clean — rain leaves mineral deposits and carries airborne debris onto glass. The article recommends twice-yearly cleaning for most coastal BC homes, with additional wipes after storm events or construction nearby.

Water-Fed Poles vs. Traditional Squeegees: Which Window Cleaning Method Works Best for Homes?
URL: /pressure-washing-articles/water-fed-poles-vs-traditional-squeegees-which-window-cleaning-method-works-best-for-homes/
A comparison of the two primary residential window cleaning methods. Water-fed poles use purified water delivered through a brush head — best for exterior glass on multiple storeys. Traditional squeegee technique offers a hand-finished result and is suited to interior glass and ground-level exterior work. Most quality window cleaners use both methods on the same property.

Why Professional Window Cleaning Matters for Surrey Storefronts
URL: /pressure-washing-articles/professional-window-cleaning-surrey-storefronts/
Storefront glass is the first impression customers form of a business. The article examines what professional cleaning achieves that in-house efforts cannot: ladder-safe access to upper panes, spot-free results from purified water, and a recurring programme that ensures consistent presentation without management overhead.

How Much Does Holiday Lighting Cost in Surrey, BC?
URL: /pressure-washing-articles/how-much-does-holiday-lighting-cost-in-surrey-bc/
A clear breakdown of the factors that shape professional holiday lighting installation pricing in the Surrey and Greater Vancouver area: roofline length, tree and landscape features, light quality, custom design, and removal and storage. The article provides realistic price ranges and explains why professional installation often costs less than the time and equipment a homeowner would invest doing it themselves.

Lighten Your Holiday Load With Professional Christmas Lighting Installation
URL: /pressure-washing-articles/lighten-your-holiday-load-with-professional-christmas-lighting-installation/
An honest account of what outsourcing holiday lighting actually feels like: no ladders, no storage, no mid-season outages. The article explains how professional installers design, install, maintain, and remove displays, and what homeowners can expect from the first consultation through to January removal.

Concrete & Paver Sealing Protects Your Hardscaped Surfaces
URL: /pressure-washing-articles/concrete-and-paver-sealing-protects-your-hardscaped-surfaces/
Salty sea air and Fraser Valley rain accelerate concrete deterioration. The article explains what sealing does: creates a barrier against UV exposure, moisture ingress, staining, and organic growth, and makes surfaces significantly easier to maintain. Covers when to seal, how often, and what to expect from the process.

A Pressure Washing Pro Will Help You Take Back Your Weekends
URL: /pressure-washing-articles/a-pressure-washing-pro-will-help-you-take-back-your-weekends/
The honest case for professional exterior maintenance, written from the perspective of someone who does it for a living. Covers what a professional brings — equipment, technique, efficiency, and knowledge of what each surface actually needs — and what it costs homeowners in time and equipment to attempt it themselves.


FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS
--------------------------

The site-wide FAQ at /faq/ covers questions across every service category: service areas, residential pressure and soft wash, window cleaning methods and cadence, concrete and paver sealing, holiday lighting, commercial and strata programmes, bookings and pricing, and insurance, safety, and practice.

Service-specific FAQ pages provide deeper detail in three areas:

House Washing FAQ — /residential-pressure-washing/house-washing/faq/
Covers: pressure vs. soft wash for siding; cladding compatibility (vinyl, cedar, Hardie, stucco, brick, painted wood); recommended frequency for coastal BC; landscaping safety; paint and surface integrity; typical job duration; multi-storey and hillside access; weather considerations; and how long results last.

Roof Cleaning FAQ — /residential-pressure-washing/roof-cleaning/faq/
Covers: shingle damage concerns and why we soft wash exclusively; what gloeocapsa magma is and why it matters; differentiating gloeocapsa from moss and lichen; when crews walk on the roof; recommended cleaning cycle for the Lower Mainland; treatment longevity and zinc/copper strip add-ons; manufacturer warranty compatibility; cleaning of metal, tile, and flat roofs; runoff and landscaping protection; and combining roof cleaning with gutter clearing.

Holiday Lighting FAQ — /christmas-lighting/faq/
Covers: booking timeline (October fills first); whether we supply lights; the bulb types and colours available; custom design; pricing factors; electrical setup and timers; mid-season service guarantee; January removal and inter-season storage; year-over-year design refinement; commercial holiday lighting; and weather-related rescheduling.
